high_consensus.description: Epic-level sequencing is foundational for any scheduler/agent; adding explicit waits-for links is low effort and prevents high-impact out-of-order Sprint 3 infra work that could bake in stale references.

Known dependency gaps without enforcement are effectively broken constraints; encoding them (or adding an explicit execution gate) is high ROI and directly prevents invalid verification/enablement tasks from running before prerequisites exist.

Self-contained beads with minimal acceptance criteria materially improve executability for both humans and agents; moderate editing cost across 21 tasks but strong payoff in reduced ambiguity and drift risk.
Data model normalization (Parent vs Dependencies) improves tooling reliability and queryability; requires some refactoring but prevents systemic confusion as the graph scales.

disputed.description: A terminal acceptance gate that aggregates all staging readiness checks is a best practice for any deployment-oriented sprint. Without it, you can have all tasks marked 'done' while the system isn't actually staging-ready.

Graduation from shadow to enabled mode logically requires a working staging environment, deploy pipeline, and audit controls. The current dependencies (only T-1.6 and T-1.8) appear to capture the application-level prerequisites but miss the infrastructure and operational prerequisites.

blockers.concern: Critical dependencies described in sprint.md are not encoded in the beads graph, so automation or human execution driven by beads may run tasks out of order.
blockers.location: Known Dependency Gaps: T-3.5, T-3.6, T-3.7; overall beads dependency graph

blockers.concern: Sprint-to-sprint dependency (Sprint 1 → Sprint 2 → Sprint 3) is stated but not represented as epic-level dependencies.
blockers.location: Epic beads (bd-1tcd, bd-3dt6, bd-2e9h) with no dependencies

blockers.concern: Multiple tasks are placeholders ('Description: see sprint.md') with no in-bead acceptance criteria.
Security/IAM scope task (T-3.3) lacks explicit constraints and there's no explicit dependency from deploy workflow to IAM scoping.
Environment isolation verification (T-3.5) is not connected to concrete isolation controls.
Staging deploy workflow (T-3.4) likely needs rollback, health checks, smoke tests, and artifact immutability.
Auth + x402 + audit verification (T-3.6) is underspecified.
Graduation protocol (T-3.7) depends on router state machine and metrics per sprint.md, but the beads graph doesn't enforce it.
Terraform safety runbook (T-3.1) is referenced but not validated by any explicit guardrails.
